reviews.review_msg: Neven has the rare ability to take a difficult subject and clarify it for anyone who's willing to put in the time and effort.  He's professional, but not cold.  He's friendly, but doesn't sacrifice professionalism.  He teaches in a concise and engaging style that probes and captures the interest of the class, and holds it until the final tutorial.

the teachers very motivated and passionate about the subject matter, but everyone was shocked after seeing their exam marks!! u mite do well on diet analysis, but ur in for a surprise for the exams! everything is memorization and it has to be VERBATIM for the exam or u lose marks even though it means the same thing...just in ur words

Professor Krallis was very passionate but his lectures were information heavy and difficult to follow. They were 2 hours full of information, that was difficult to remember and understand. His exams could be on anything from his lectures. My advice for the tests is to keep studying all the study questions for each week and keep up with readings.
He is too harsh with grading the exams. Though, the lectures seem to be interesting. This course is unique in which six different profs are lecturing in it.

He was one of several profs that simultaneously taught this course; I am glad that he didn't make this class buy any textbooks! But I struggled to complete a 15 minute quiz on the same day of a three hour lecture which felt overwhelming. I also didn't like his sarcasm since it made it difficult for me to learn.
